fact: April 9 is exactly 99 days into the year! That means there are 266 days left to go - almost like the year is doing a countdown.
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is typically prime time for cherry blossoms to explode into pink clouds that look like cotton candy grew on trees overnight.
Spring energy is peaking in northern regions, and young rabbits, lambs, and deer are often literally hopping mad with excitement around this time.
The sun is climbing higher every day, and by April 9, daylight usually lasts about 13 hours in most northern places.
In Australia and other southern places, autumn is getting serious - leaves are typically turning brilliant orange and red like nature's own fireworks show.
Aries season is still ruling the zodiac until April 19, which means the sky is supposedly buzzing with ram-like energy and new beginnings.
Tree sap is flowing like crazy right now in maple forests of North America, making this the perfect time for syrup-making - trees are basically bleeding sugar water!
In northern regions, the last stubborn patches of snow are typically melting into muddy, squelchy messes that make the most satisfying splashing sounds.
Migrating birds are often flying overhead in massive V-shaped formations, like living arrows pointing toward their summer homes thousands of miles away.
Dandelions are usually preparing to launch their fuzzy seed parachutes into the air around this time, turning every lawn into a natural helicopter landing pad.
In cold mountain areas, avalanche season is typically at its most dangerous because warming temperatures make snow as unstable as a house of cards.
